ocr.text: The analysis presented in the accompanying table elucidates the odds ratios (OR) associated with various factors influencing
outcomes at individual sites. Specifically, the metrics for Model 1OR and Model 2OR provide insights into the comparative
effectiveness of interventions under differing conditions. By employing a stratified approach, this analysis facilitates a nuanced

understanding of how specific variables impact site-level performance. 

The first model, denoted as Model 1OR (95% CI),
captures the baseline influence of selected predictors, while the second model, Model 2OR (95% CI), incorporates additional
covariates to refine the estimates.

At individual sites

This layered methodology not only enhances the robustness of the findings but also allows for a more comprehensive

evaluation of the underlying dynamics at play. 

Notably, the confidence intervals (CI) accompanying each odds ratio offer
critical insights into the statistical significance and reliability of the estimates, underscoring the importance of considering
variability in the data. As stakeholders assess these results, it is imperative to recognize how these odds ratios can inform
strategic decision-making and policy formulation tailored to the unique contexts of each site. The implications of these findings
extend beyond mere numbers, contributing to a deeper understanding of the operational landscape.
